variants.global: I write numbered steps before starting a new multi-step chore.
variants.ghana: Fresh many-step tasks get a numbered list from me first.
variants.west_africa: New complex chores start with ordered notes.

variants.global: I like open-ended days where tasks can reshuffle freely.
variants.ghana: Flexible days with reshuffle room feel better than rigid slots.
variants.west_africa: Open schedules please me more than fixed minute maps.

variants.global: Clear written rules lower my stress in shared spaces.
variants.ghana: Posted plain rules calm me in shared work or living areas.
variants.west_africa: Visible simple rules ease me in common spaces.

variants.global: If materials are missing, I improvise with what is nearby.
variants.ghana: Absent tools lead me to nearby substitutes.
variants.west_africa: Gaps in supplies spark on-the-spot swaps.

variants.global: I pack bags the night before a morning trip.
variants.ghana: Evening-before packing is my habit for early travel.
variants.west_africa: Morning trips get bags ready the prior night.

variants.global: Surprise visits feel disruptive when I had a fixed task list.
variants.ghana: Unplanned guests clash with a tight written list day.
variants.west_africa: Pop-in visits jar me on list-heavy days.

variants.global: I reset objects to the same spots so I can find them again.
variants.ghana: Things return to fixed homes so search is short.
variants.west_africa: Stable object homes speed finding.

variants.global: I question a rule that blocks safety or fairness even if it is written.
variants.ghana: Written rules that risk harm or unfairness get a spoken question from me.
variants.west_africa: I raise concerns about bad posted rules.

variants.global: Doing steps out of order on purpose feels uncomfortable if order matters for quality.
variants.ghana: Wrong order on purpose feels off when order affects outcome.
variants.west_africa: Sequence jumps unsettle me when sequence affects results.

variants.global: I prefer a recipe or pattern the first time I try a new craft.
variants.ghana: First attempt at a craft wants a pattern in hand.
variants.west_africa: New craft starts with a model to copy.

variants.global: I estimate time for each subtask before I begin.
variants.ghana: Subtask minutes get guessed before start.
variants.west_africa: I slice total time across parts ahead of work.

variants.global: I enjoy discovering the path while moving rather than mapping everything first.
variants.ghana: Forward motion with partial map feels fun.
variants.west_africa: Full pre-map is less fun than discover-as-I-go.

variants.global: I start multi-step chores without writing steps first.
variants.ghana: Many-step jobs begin straight from the head without lists.
variants.west_africa: I launch complex tasks without numbered prep.

variants.global: Tight schedules with little reshuffle room feel better to me than loose days.
variants.ghana: Minute-tight plans beat fully open days for my comfort.
variants.west_africa: I prefer heavy structure over floating tasks.

variants.global: Posted rules feel neutral or slightly stressful rather than calming.
variants.ghana: Wall rules neither calm nor excite me; slight stress.
variants.west_africa: Written shared rules add little comfort.

variants.global: Missing materials stall me until the right tool appears.
variants.ghana: Without exact supplies I pause rather than swap.
variants.west_africa: Wrong-tool work is hard to start.

variants.global: I pack on the morning of travel rather than the night before.
variants.ghana: Travel bags get packed at wake time.
variants.west_africa: Same-day morning pack is my norm.

variants.global: Surprise visits feel welcome even on a fixed task day.
variants.ghana: Unplanned guests fit fine beside a list.
variants.west_africa: Drop-in visitors sit well with planned chores.

variants.global: I leave tools where I last used them and search later.
variants.ghana: Objects rest where dropped until needed again.
variants.west_africa: Floating placement is my habit.

variants.global: I keep the same tool layout between sessions so I can grab fast.
variants.ghana: Tools return to the same layout each time so my hand finds them fast.
variants.west_africa: I reset my workspace layout the same way between sessions.

variants.global: Order of steps rarely matters to me if the end result looks fine.
variants.ghana: End quality OK means order felt flexible to me.
variants.west_africa: Step sequence is loose in my mind.

variants.global: I try a new craft first by experimenting without a pattern.
variants.ghana: Pattern-free first trial is my craft style.
variants.west_africa: New making starts with free play.

variants.global: I begin work and discover time needs as I go.
variants.ghana: Clock needs reveal during the job.
variants.west_africa: No upfront subtask time guesses.

variants.global: I want the full map before any first step.
variants.ghana: Complete preview before motion feels required.
variants.west_africa: All-at-once planning precedes action.

variants.global: Shared tidy zones matter less to me than finishing the job.
variants.ghana: End finish beats mid-way neat placement for me.
variants.west_africa: Completion tops strict tidiness.

variants.global: When the method must change mid-task, I switch without long complaint.
variants.ghana: A required method swap mid-job gets a quick shift from me.
variants.west_africa: Forced path change mid-task draws little grumble time.

variants.global: Trying a new route to a familiar place feels refreshing.
variants.ghana: A different path to the same end feels like a treat.
variants.west_africa: Unfamiliar roads to known places please me.

variants.global: I feel uneasy the first day a daily routine is interrupted.
variants.ghana: Day one of a broken routine feels off in my body.
variants.west_africa: Routine break day one unsettles me.

variants.global: If a tool breaks, I look for another object that can do the job.
variants.ghana: Tool failure sends me hunting for a stand-in object.
variants.west_africa: Broken gear triggers substitute search.

variants.global: New faces in a steady work group take me a meeting or two to adjust to.
variants.ghana: Fresh members in a fixed team need a couple meets from me.
variants.west_africa: Steady crew changes need warm-up meets.

variants.global: Weather shifts my outdoor plan and I rebuild the plan fast.
variants.ghana: Rain or wind reshapes outdoor work with quick replan.
variants.west_africa: Sky change gets a fast alternate plan.

variants.global: I revisit old habits even when a new habit would work better.
variants.ghana: Better new habit still loses to old groove sometimes.
variants.west_africa: Comfort habit wins over improved new pattern at times.

variants.global: I need the reason for a rule change before I accept the new rule.
variants.ghana: Rule swaps want a clear why from me.
variants.west_africa: New rules need rationale to stick.

variants.global: Updated software layout slows me down until I explore every corner.
variants.ghana: Fresh app layout needs a full click tour before speed returns.
variants.west_africa: UI redesign needs exploration time.

variants.global: Canceled plans free me to do something else useful quickly.
variants.ghana: Plan drops become quick slots for other useful acts.
variants.west_africa: Cancelation redirects me fast to next useful thing.

variants.global: I sample unfamiliar food willingly when ingredients are named.
variants.ghana: Named parts make unknown dishes OK to taste.
variants.west_africa: Ingredient labels open me to new plates.

variants.global: I return smoothly to a routine after a holiday break.
variants.ghana: Post-holiday rhythm snaps back for me.
variants.west_africa: After time off, old daily pattern returns easily.

variants.global: Method change mid-task frustrates me for a noticeable stretch.
variants.ghana: Mid-job method swap costs me mood time.
variants.west_africa: Path change mid-task irritates before I move on.

variants.global: A new route to a familiar place feels like unnecessary risk.
variants.ghana: Different roads to the same place feel risky not fun.
variants.west_africa: Alternate paths feel needless.

variants.global: First interrupted routine day feels fine to me.
variants.ghana: Routine break day one feels normal.
variants.west_africa: Day-one routine gap does not shake me.

variants.global: Tool break stops the job until the exact tool is fixed or replaced.
variants.ghana: Without the right tool I halt rather than substitute.
variants.west_africa: Exact-tool dependence on failure.

variants.global: New people in a steady group feel instantly normal to me.
variants.ghana: Fresh faces slot in immediately for me.
variants.west_africa: Instant comfort with new team members.

variants.global: Weather change leaves me stuck without a new plan for a while.
variants.ghana: Sky shift freezes my planning for a bit.
variants.west_africa: Weather turns create plan blanks.

variants.global: I adopt a better new habit quickly when I see it works.
variants.ghana: Shown better habit swaps in fast for me.
variants.west_africa: Evidence of improvement drives fast habit swap.

variants.global: I accept a rule change on brief notice without needing a long reason.
variants.ghana: Short notice rule change sits fine without deep why.
variants.west_africa: New rule on brief word works for me.

variants.global: Updated software feels intuitive right away.
variants.ghana: New layout clicks immediately.
variants.west_africa: Redesign speeds me up from day one.

variants.global: Canceled plans leave me drifting unsure what to do next.
variants.ghana: Dropped plans create empty unsure minutes.
variants.west_africa: Cancelation empties my next move.

variants.global: Unfamiliar food makes me refuse even when ingredients are named.
variants.ghana: Unknown dishes get a no even with parts listed.
variants.west_africa: Named unfamiliar plates still get declined.

variants.global: After a long break, rebuilding routine takes many days.
variants.ghana: Holiday return stretches routine rebuild across days.
variants.west_africa: Slow return to rhythm after time away.

variants.global: I combine odd objects creatively when standard tools are gone.
variants.ghana: No standard tool sparks creative mashups from me.
variants.west_africa: Odd combos finish jobs without proper gear.
